title: Fail model frontier loudly when no labeled records exist
description:
Observed behavior: model frontier can complete with no_data rows when the feedback-derived record set is empty.

Expected behavior: because this procedure compares cost vs accuracy, a run with no labeled records should fail loudly before dispatching evaluations. Silent no_data completion is misleading in prototype mode.

Feature: Model frontier requires labeled comparison records

Scenario: Score has no labeled records
Given a model frontier run for a score with no eligible feedback-derived labeled records
When the procedure prepares evaluations
Then the procedure fails with a clear message that no labeled records are available
And it does not dispatch candidate evaluations.

Scenario: Score has labeled records
Given a model frontier run with eligible labeled records
When the procedure prepares evaluations
Then it dispatches evaluations for the planned variants.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: User clarified that silent no_data completion is undesirable. Treating the previous no_data row behavior as a soft fallback and replacing it with a loud procedure failure when no eligible labeled feedback-derived records exist. Keep strict associated-dataset reuse check because stale dataset reuse was the source of misleading regular evaluation dispatch.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Changed implementation: removed no_data result-row behavior and made the procedure fail loudly when eligible_feedback_rows <= 0 before evaluation dispatch. Kept the stricter requirement that associated regression datasets have feedback_target_hash before reuse, so stale datasets are not used as a substitute for missing labeled records. Validation: focused frontier/runtime/TactusScore tests passed, procedure YAML parsed, py_compile passed.
